recipes.title: Koshari
recipes.description: Egypt's beloved street food: a tower of rice, lentils, pasta and chickpeas crowned with crisp onions and tangy tomato sauce.
steps: Slice onions thinly and fry in oil for 20 minutes until deep brown and crisp; reserve oil.
Simmer lentils for 15 minutes until just tender, then drain.
Toast vermicelli in 2 tablespoons of the onion oil, add rinsed rice, lentils, salt and 500 ml water.
Cover and simmer 18 minutes; rest 10 minutes off the heat.
Make the tomato sauce: fry crushed garlic in oil, add passata, cumin, coriander, chili and salt; simmer 15 minutes, then stir in vinegar.
Boil pasta separately, layer pasta, rice mixture and chickpeas in bowls and top with sauce and crisp onions.

recipes.title: Ful Medames
recipes.description: Slow-cooked fava beans mashed with garlic, lemon, olive oil and cumin — Egypt's iconic breakfast.
steps: Soak the fava beans in cold water overnight.
Drain and cover with fresh water in a heavy pot, simmering very gently for 4 hours.
Top up water as needed and cook until the beans are completely soft.
Mash about half the beans against the side of the pot for a chunky texture.
Stir in crushed garlic, salt, cumin, lemon juice and olive oil.
Top with diced tomato, parsley and a final drizzle of olive oil; serve with pita.

recipes.title: Ta'ameya
recipes.description: Egyptian falafel made with skinned dried fava beans and herbs, fried into emerald-green crisp patties.
steps: Soak split favas in cold water for 24 hours, then drain very thoroughly.
Pulse favas, onion, leek, garlic, herbs, cumin, coriander and salt to a vivid green coarse paste.
Stir in baking soda and chill the mixture for 30 minutes.
Shape into small patties and press sesame seeds onto both sides.
Heat oil to 170°C and fry patties in batches for 3-4 minutes until dark green-gold and crisp.
Drain on paper and serve in pita with tahini, salad and pickles.

recipes.title: Molokhia
recipes.description: Egyptian-style finely chopped jute leaf soup with rabbit or chicken, finished with a sizzling garlic and coriander tarka.
steps: Simmer chicken with onion, bay leaves, cardamom and salt for 60 minutes; strain and reserve broth.
Defrost the molokhia leaves and add to 1 liter of strained simmering broth.
Cook gently for 10 minutes without boiling vigorously.
Crush garlic with a pinch of salt and the ground coriander.
Heat ghee in a small pan, fry the garlic-coriander paste until golden and fragrant (the ta'aleya).
Tip the sizzling tarka into the soup, stir in lemon juice and serve with rice and chicken.

recipes.title: Mahshi Cromb
recipes.description: Egyptian stuffed cabbage rolls of seasoned rice, herbs and tomato, simmered with garlic and cumin in lemony broth.
steps: Boil the cored cabbage in salted water, peeling off leaves as they soften.
Trim thick ribs and halve large leaves along the spine.
Mix rinsed rice with chopped tomato, tomato paste, herbs, ghee, cumin and salt.
Place a finger of filling on each leaf and roll tightly into thin cigars.
Layer rolls in a heavy pot with crushed garlic between layers.
Pour over water mixed with lemon juice, weight with a plate and simmer covered for 50 minutes.

recipes.title: Feteer Meshaltet
recipes.description: Many-layered Egyptian flaky pastry, brushed with ghee until paper-thin, baked golden and served plain or filled.
steps: Mix flour, salt and warm water into a soft dough; knead 10 minutes until silky.
Divide into 8 balls, coat in oil and rest 1 hour.
Stretch each ball on an oiled surface into a paper-thin large sheet.
Brush generously with melted ghee, fold each sheet into a small square and stack.
Roll the stack into a 30 cm round and place on a baking tray.
Bake at 220°C for 25 minutes until deep golden and very flaky.

recipes.title: Hamam Mahshi
recipes.description: Egyptian roasted pigeon stuffed with freekeh, onions and warm spices, traditionally cooked in a clay tagine.
steps: Soak the freekeh in cold water for 30 minutes, then drain.
Brown chopped onion in ghee, add freekeh, spices and salt and toast 3 minutes.
Pour in 400 ml stock and simmer 10 minutes until almost tender; cool.
Stuff each pigeon with freekeh and truss the legs together.
Brown the pigeons in ghee, then nestle in a heavy pot with remaining stock and lemon juice.
Cover and braise at 180°C for 60 minutes until tender, then crisp under a hot grill for 5 minutes.

recipes.title: Roz Bel Laban
recipes.description: Egyptian rice pudding cooked low and slow with milk, sugar and a hint of vanilla, finished with cinnamon and pistachios.
steps: Rinse rice and simmer with water for 15 minutes until water is absorbed.
Pour in 800 ml of milk and cook gently for 25 minutes, stirring often.
Whisk cornstarch into the remaining milk, then stir into the rice with sugar.
Cook another 8 minutes until creamy and slightly thickened.
Stir in vanilla and ladle into bowls; chill for 2 hours.
Top with crushed pistachios and a dusting of cinnamon before serving.

recipes.title: Umm Ali
recipes.description: Egyptian bread pudding of buttery puff pastry baked in sweetened milk with nuts and raisins, served bubbling and golden.
steps: Bake the puff pastry at 200°C for 15 minutes until puffed and golden, then crumble.
Spread the crumbled pastry into a baking dish with the nuts, coconut and raisins.
Heat milk and sugar in a pot until just steaming, stirring to dissolve.
Pour the warm milk over the pastry and let it absorb for 5 minutes.
Whip the cream and dollop over the surface, then dust with cinnamon.
Bake at 220°C for 12-15 minutes until bubbling and the cream is golden; serve immediately.

recipes.title: Basbousa
recipes.description: Tender semolina cake soaked with citrus syrup and topped with toasted almonds, a beloved Egyptian sweet.
steps: Make syrup by simmering sugar, water and lemon juice for 10 minutes; stir in rose water and cool.
Brush a tray with tahini and dust with semolina.
Mix semolina, sugar, baking powder, yogurt and melted ghee into a soft batter.
Spread into the tray and score into diamonds; press an almond into each diamond.
Bake at 190°C for 30-35 minutes until deep golden.
Pour cool syrup over the hot cake and rest 30 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Ful Sudani
recipes.description: Egyptian-style spiced peanut soup, creamy and lightly tomatoey, eaten as a winter starter with bread.
steps: Sauté chopped onion and garlic in olive oil for 5 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste, cumin, coriander and chili flakes and cook 1 minute.
Whisk in the peanut butter and pour over the water.
Simmer 15 minutes until the soup is creamy and rich.
Season with salt and lemon juice to balance.
Ladle into bowls and serve with toasted flatbread.

recipes.title: Macarona Bechamel
recipes.description: Egyptian baked pasta layered with spiced minced meat and a thick béchamel, golden and bubbling on top.
steps: Boil pasta in salted water for 8 minutes, drain and toss with a little oil.
Brown the beef with chopped onion, tomato paste, allspice, cinnamon and salt.
Make béchamel: melt butter, whisk in flour, then milk; cook to a thick sauce, season with nutmeg and salt.
Whisk a little hot sauce into the egg, then stir back into the béchamel off heat.
Layer half the pasta in a baking dish, spread the meat, then remaining pasta and béchamel on top.
Bake at 200°C for 25 minutes until deep golden and bubbling; rest 10 minutes before slicing.

recipes.title: Sayadeya
recipes.description: Egyptian fisherman's spiced rice with caramelized onions and pan-seared fish, dressed with fresh tomato salsa.
steps: Slice onions and fry in olive oil for 25 minutes until very dark; reserve some for garnish.
Stir in tomato paste and cook 1 minute, then blend half with stock for amber broth.
Toast rinsed rice in remaining onion oil with cumin, turmeric and salt.
Pour in the dark stock, cover and simmer 18 minutes; rest 10 minutes off heat.
Season fish with salt and lemon, then pan-fry skin-side down until crisp and just cooked.
Mound rice on a platter, lay fish on top, scatter reserved onions and parsley and serve with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Aish Baladi
recipes.description: Egypt's wholewheat pita: a hot puffed flatbread with a hollow pocket and a flavor of toasted bran, made daily across the country.
steps: Whisk flours, yeast and salt in a bowl, then add water and mix to a soft dough.
Knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic, then cover and rise 1 hour.
Divide into 8 balls, dust with wheat bran and rest 15 minutes.
Roll each ball into a 16 cm disc and let proof another 20 minutes.
Preheat a baking stone or heavy tray at 250°C for 30 minutes.
Bake the discs in batches for 3 minutes until they puff and the underside is freckled.

recipes.title: Hawawshi
recipes.description: Spiced minced meat stuffed inside Egyptian baladi bread and baked or pan-grilled until the bread is crisp and the meat is juicy.
steps: Finely chop onion, tomato, chili and parsley and squeeze out excess liquid.
Knead the chopped vegetables with the minced beef, allspice, cumin and salt until cohesive.
Slit each pita open like a pocket without cutting through.
Spread the meat mixture into a thin even layer inside each pita.
Brush both sides with olive oil and bake at 230°C for 18 minutes until very crisp.
Cut into wedges and serve hot with tahini and pickles.

recipes.title: Dukkah
recipes.description: Egyptian dry condiment of toasted nuts, sesame, coriander and cumin — eat with bread dipped in olive oil.
steps: Toast the hazelnuts at 180°C for 8 minutes until fragrant; rub off any skins.
Toast sesame seeds in a dry pan until pale gold, then tip onto a plate.
Toast coriander, cumin and fennel seeds 2 minutes until aromatic.
Pulse all the ingredients with salt and pepper to a coarse, sandy texture.
Be careful not to over-process or it will turn to butter.
Store in a jar and serve in a small bowl with olive oil and bread for dipping.
